reason_for_decision: The article passes because it explains the narrow-versus-wide FOV trade in task terms and ties optic choice to cue quality, context, and verification workflow.
strengths: The article is strong at separating first-look contextual verification from later narrow-view discrimination, which gives it real system-design depth.
It ties field of view to target scale, cue quality, stabilization, and acquisition tolerance instead of reducing the subject to zoom preference.
The procurement-question section gives readers practical ways to specify FOV-dependent performance rather than relying on vague range claims.
summary_assessment: This is a strong technical explainer with good decision value. It gives a disciplined framework for choosing verification optics without falling into generic camera-buying advice.
